compiled_state.title: review-workflow hash capture uses wrong method causing record-review.sh mismatches
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
During the 2026-04-04 debug-everything session, the review orchestrator repeatedly failed record-review.sh with 'diff hash mismatch — code changed between review dispatch and recording' errors across multiple passes (passes 4, 5, 6). Root cause: the orchestrator captured the diff hash using 'git diff HEAD | sha256sum' (or 'git diff --cached | sha256sum'), but record-review.sh validates against the output of compute-diff-hash.sh — which applies an exclusion pathspec allowlist (excluding .tickets-tracker/**, docs/**, .claude/docs/**, *.png, etc.) before hashing. These two methods produce different hashes for the same staged state, causing every record-review.sh call to fail.

Resolution in-session: discovered compute-diff-hash.sh is the canonical method; used 'bash plugins/dso/hooks/compute-diff-hash.sh' directly to get the correct hash before calling record-review.sh.

Root causes:
1. REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md Step 0 does not specify compute-diff-hash.sh as the hash capture method — it says to 'capture the diff hash' without naming the script.
2. There is no shared utility shim (.claude/scripts/dso compute-diff-hash or similar) making the canonical script discoverable from the host project context.
3. The orchestrator's natural instinct is 'git diff | sha256sum' which is wrong.

Impact: 6 review passes instead of ~2; ~90 minutes of extra wall-clock time on a single commit due to repeated review agent dispatches.

Proposed fixes:
1. Update REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md Step 0 to explicitly call 'DIFF_HASH=$(bash "$PLUGIN_ROOT/hooks/compute-diff-hash.sh")' and add a note that git diff | sha256sum is NOT equivalent.
2. Add compute-diff-hash.sh to the .claude/scripts/dso shim registry so it is callable as '.claude/scripts/dso compute-diff-hash.sh' from the worktree context.
3. Add an inline comment to compute-diff-hash.sh header explaining WHY it differs from plain git diff (exclusion pathspecs), so any future reader understands the invariant.

Secondary friction (same session): 'git stash' used for diagnosis unstaged all 18 staged files, requiring manual re-staging. Add a KNOWN-ISSUES entry: never use git stash when staged files need to be preserved; use git diff --cached > /tmp/patch.diff + git stash --keep-index instead.

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral. Review workflow Step 0 does not specify compute-diff-hash.sh as the hash capture method.
comments.author: Main
